predicted_questions.question_text: Thanks, good morning — two quick ones on yields. First, you laid out 2026 Net Yield growth of 1.5%–2.5%: can you give us a rough split of that range between ticket/APD, onboard/pre‑cruise ancillaries, and itinerary/mix (please quantify in bps or % and vs. 2019 if possible)? Second, you said booked load factor is within historical ranges but APD is at a record — can you quantify APD versus 2019 and give a $/night or $/APD figure for pre‑cruise spend so we can separate ticket versus onboard contributions in our model?
predicted_questions.rationale: Robin often presses on yield composition and load factor (recurring_concerns.core_topics[0]) and will ask for bps/$ splits; the presentation disclosed Net Yield guidance of 1.5%–2.5%, record APD and that >50% of onboard revenue is booked pre‑cruise, leaving open how much of yield is ticket vs onboard and how this compares to 2019.

predicted_questions.question_text: Thanks — and on costs/fuel: you called out almost 400bps of Q2 cost headwinds from extra dry‑dock days and crew travel and said Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel are roughly flat for the year — how much of that quarterly headwind is timing versus structural (please give bps or $/share) and what run‑rate of dry‑dock‑related cost pressure should we model beyond 2026? As a follow up, please reconcile the fuel headwind references — Jason mentioned ~$0.62 per share, the slides/discussion referenced $0.74 and you said ~59%–60% hedged — what fuel price ($/bbl or $/MT) is embedded in the $17.10–$17.50 guide and which per‑share fuel headwind should we put in our model?
predicted_questions.rationale: Robin frequently distinguishes transitional vs structural cost drivers and asks for quantified dry‑dock impacts (recurring_concerns.core_topics[1]); management highlighted ~400bps Q2 headwind from dry docks and gave mixed fuel‑headwind figures (CEO $0.62, CFO $0.74 and 59%/60% hedged), so Robin will press for precise bps/$ reconciliation and what is baked into guidance.
